items.title: Apple fixes bug that cops used to extract deleted chat messages from iPhones
items.content: The iPhone and iPad bug allowed law enforcement using forensic tools to read messages that had long been deleted by the Signal app.

items.title: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple: Here’s a look at his 15-year legacy, from new products and services to China expansion
items.content: Cook, who joined Apple in 1998, succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011 and went on to transform Apple into a $4 trillion powerhouse.

items.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
items.content: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

items.title: Apple’s Cal AI crackdown signals it’s still policing the App Store
items.content: Apple tells TechCrunch Cal AI was removed not for web payments alone, but for deceptive billing, manipulative tactics, and App Store rule violations.

items.title: Microsoft offers buyout for up to 7% of US employees
items.content: If a worker's years of service at Microsoft plus their age equals 70 or more, they will be eligible for a voluntary retirement buyout.

items.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
items.content: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vuln

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.content: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connectio

items.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
items.content: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

items.title: How Kevin Warsh Could Shrink the Fed’s Footprint in Financial Markets
items.content: President Trump’s nominee to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve wants to overhaul the central bank, including its more than $6 trillion balance sheet.

items.title: Why Everyone, Including Trump, Is Talking About Airline Mergers
items.content: A few carriers are making a lot of money while many are struggling. President Trump and industry executives seem to think mergers may be the answer.

items.title: Ignoring the War Has Been Working for Long-Term Investors
items.content: The U.S. stock market’s quick rebound this month may be fragile, but riding out the market’s disconcerting waves has been a sound strategy.

items.title: A.I. Is Forcing More Belt-Tightening at Big Tech
items.content: The growing expense of keeping up in the artificial intelligence race is driving technology companies, young and old, to cut costs.
